data.title: Asian countries face energy crisis as Middle East L.N.G. supply dwindles. Coal use spikes, consumption drops.
data.description: As the possibility of a disruption in shipments looms large, Asian nations, the primary purchasers of liquefied natural gas from the Middle East, have ramped up their usage of coal while scaling back on gas consumption.

data.title: $290M exits Bitcoin ETFs due to geopolitical tensions, fading ceasefire hopes, and end-of-quarter rebalancing.
data.description: Last week, experts linked the outflows of BTC ETF to global political tensions, diminishing prospects of a ceasefire, and the quarter-end rebalancing.

data.title: Crypto Fraudster Sam Bankman-Fried Showers Praises On Trump, Says He's The First President To Recognize 'Strategic Potential' Of Crypto And AI
data.description: Sam Bankman-Fried, a jailed fraudster in the cryptocurrency realm, commended President Donald Trump for his vision in acknowledging the vast potential of cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence. Known as SBF, he lauded Trump’s recent statements at the Future Investment Initiative Priority Summit in Miami where the President lauded Bitcoin as a robust asset and noted the increasing preference for cryptocurrency payments among the public. SBF emphasized the shift towards AI agents engaging in cryptocurrency transactions, declaring that crypto represents the future of AI-related payments. He highlighted Trump as the pioneering president in recognizing the strategic importance of both cryptocurrency and AI. This information was reported on Benzinga.com.

data.title: WuXi Biologics Honored with CDMO Leadership Awards for Ninth Consecutive Year
data.description: In Shanghai on March 30, 2026, WuXi Biologics, trading under the symbol 2269.HK, a prominent worldwide Contract Research, Development and Manufacturing Organization (CRDMO), was declared the recipient of the "Biologics CDMO of the Year" award (Large CDMOs) in the 2026 CDMO Leadership Awards. This recognition marks another accolade for the company, solidifying its position as a leader in the industry.

data.title: Rio Tinto provides Iron Ore update following Tropical Cyclone Narelle
data.description: After Tropical Cyclone Narelle hit Western Australia's Pilbara region, Rio Tinto's iron ore port operations have now reopened. The closure of the company's four Pilbara iron ore port terminals began on 24 March 2026, with ship loading at East Intercourse Island, Parker Point, and Cape Lambert B restarting on 28 March. Fortunately, all Rio Tinto employees remained safe throughout the weather event. Repairs are currently underway for the damage sustained at Cape Lambert A during the cyclone.

data.title: Trader profits $67k by capitalizing on UFC announcer's mistake, buying $676 of one-cent shares in Tyrell Fortune.
data.description: A trader on Polymarket swiftly purchased $676 worth of one-cent shares in Tyrell Fortune when it became clear that the UFC announcer had erroneously declared the wrong winner of the fight.

data.title: Sivers Semiconductors Announces Availability of Daybreak™ 5G/6G Advanced ICs For Emerging FR3 Applications
data.description: New beamforming chipsets operating within the 7-15ghz range are projected to tap into a lucrative market opportunity worth $1.3 billion in the base station and consumer premise equipment sectors. The Swedish company Sivers Semiconductors AB, known for its expertise in photonics and wireless technology, is leading the charge in this innovation. This development is anticipated to significantly expand the serviceable available market (SAM) in these segments.

data.title: Outcome of Subscription to Coop Pank AS Unsecured Subordinated Bonds
data.description: Coop Pank AS recently concluded its public offering of unsecured subordinated bonds on Friday, 27 March. Throughout the offering, a total of 10,000 unsecured subordinated bonds under the name EUR 6,25 Coop Pank bond 26-2037 were available. These bonds hold a nominal value of EUR 1,000 each, a maturity date of 1 April 2037, and a fixed interest rate of 6.25% per annum, which is paid quarterly. If oversubscribed, Coop Pank had the option to expand the offering by an additional 10,000 bonds. It's important to note that the offering was exclusive to Estonia and did not extend to any other jurisdiction.
